pmda_guidelines: PMDA R&D Promotion / Orphan Drug Framework

requirements.description: Definitions of 'labeling' and 'label' — governs misbranding determinations
Mandatory label information SHALL appear; failure to reveal material facts = misleading (1.21)
Controls for closed systems: validation, audit trails, operational checks, authority checks, device checks
Electronic signatures SHALL be unique to one individual; SHALL NOT be reused or reassigned
Electronic signatures SHALL employ at least two distinct identification components (e.g., ID code + password)
No human subject SHALL be involved in research without legally effective informed consent
Informed consent MUST include 8 basic elements and 6 additional elements when appropriate
Applicant MUST submit financial certification or disclosure statement for each clinical investigator
If compensation affected by study outcome OR investigator had proprietary interest OR significant equity interest OR significant payments: MUST disclose
No clinical investigation SHALL be initiated without IRB review and approval
IRB SHALL conduct continuing review at intervals no less than once per year

part_58.title: Good Laboratory Practice for Nonclinical Studies

requirements.description: Testing facility management SHALL designate a study director and quality assurance unit
Quality assurance unit SHALL maintain copy of master schedule and maintain copies of all protocols and amendments
Final report MUST include: study objectives, methods, results, QA statement, dated signatures of study director and contributing scientists
Ophthalmic preparations SHALL be sterile at time of filling and closing
All aqueous-based drug products for oral inhalation MUST be manufactured to be sterile
Drug label MUST bear name and place of business of manufacturer, packer, or distributor
Adequate directions for use SHALL cover all conditions for which drug is intended
Established name MUST accompany proprietary name on label per FD&C Act 502(e)
Expiration date MUST appear on immediate container AND outer package
Bar code (NDC) SHALL meet EAN/UCC or HIBCC standards on drug labels
Rx labeling MUST contain summary of essential scientific information; MUST be informative, accurate, not promotional; MUST conform to 201.57 format

FPI requires 17 numbered sections: Boxed Warning, Indications, Dosage, Dosage Forms, Contraindications, Warnings/Precautions, Adverse Reactions, Drug Interactions, Specific Populations (Pregnancy/Lactation/Pediatric/Geriatric), Overdosage, Description, Clinical Pharmacology, Nonclinical Tox, Clinical Studies, References, How Supplied, Patient Counseling
highlights_requirements: HOPI MUST NOT exceed 20 lines for boxed warning summary
Recent Major Changes section MUST list changes in past 12 months
Adverse reactions listed MUST have >=10% incidence or as specified

requirements.description: Systemic antibacterial drugs MUST bear antibiotic stewardship statement
OTC Drug Facts labeling: content order MUST follow prescribed sequence; warnings section MUST include all applicable subsections
Aluminum in LVP/TPN SHALL NOT exceed 25 mcg/L; manufacturers MUST use validated assay
All domestic and foreign drug establishments MUST register annually with FDA
Quality control unit SHALL have authority to approve/reject all components, in-process materials, and finished products
Automated equipment SHALL be routinely calibrated, inspected, and checked per written program
Each lot of components SHALL be tested for identity and conformity to specifications
Drug product containers SHALL be safe, not reactive, additive, or absorptive
Written procedures SHALL be designed to assure drug products have identity, strength, quality, and purity
In-process specifications SHALL be derived from previous acceptable process averages and variability estimates
Appropriate written procedures designed to prevent microbiological contamination SHALL be established and followed
Expiration dating SHALL be determined from stability testing; no drug SHALL be distributed past expiration date
Each batch SHALL be tested for identity, strength, quality, and purity per sampling plan providing statistical confidence
Written stability testing program SHALL include sample size and test intervals based on statistical criteria
Records SHALL be retained for at least 1 year after expiration date of the batch
Laboratory records SHALL include complete data derived from all tests; suitability of methods SHALL be verified under actual conditions of use
Clinical investigations SHALL consist of Phase 1 (pharmacology/safety), Phase 2 (effectiveness/dose), Phase 3 (expanded safety/effectiveness)

IND MUST contain: cover sheet (Form FDA-1571), TOC, introductory statement, general investigational plan, investigator's brochure, clinical protocol, CMC information, pharmacology/toxicology information, previous human experience
Protocol amendments MUST be submitted for new Phase 2/3 studies and significant changes to existing protocols
IND safety reports: sponsor MUST report suspected adverse reactions that are both serious AND unexpected within 15 calendar days; fatal/life-threatening within 7 calendar days
Sponsor responsibilities: select qualified investigators, ensure proper monitoring, ensure protocol compliance, maintain IND records
Investigator responsibilities: conduct study per protocol, maintain adequate records, report adverse events promptly

part_314.title: Applications for FDA Approval to Market a New Drug

requirements.description: NDA MUST contain: (a) application form, (b) TOC, (c) labeling, (d)(1) CMC, (d)(2) nonclinical pharmacology/tox, (d)(3) human PK and bioavailability, (d)(5) clinical data, (e) samples, (f) case report forms/tabulations

subsections.d1_cmc: M3 — full description of drug substance and drug product including manufacturing, controls, specifications, stability
subsections.d2_nonclinical: M4 — adequate nonclinical studies demonstrating safety, GLP statement required per Part 58
subsections.d5_clinical: M5 — adequate and well-controlled clinical studies per 314.126

requirements.description: Post-approval changes: Major=Prior Approval Supplement; Moderate=CBE-30; Minor=Annual Report
Annual reports MUST be filed within 60 days of anniversary date of approval
ANDA MUST contain: bioequivalence data, labeling, CMC information, patent certifications per 314.52
Complete Response Letter: FDA WILL issue CRL describing ALL deficiencies; applicant MUST respond within 1 year or application may be considered withdrawn

FDA SHALL refuse NDA approval if: insufficient evidence of safety/effectiveness, inadequate labeling, manufacturing methods inadequate, lack of bioavailability data, or lack of substantial evidence from adequate and well-controlled studies

Adequate and well-controlled studies MUST include: clear objectives, comparison with control, subject selection criteria, method of assignment, measures to minimize bias, methods of assessment, adequate analysis of results
Bioequivalence MUST be demonstrated for drug products meeting criteria in 320.21(b) — oral dosage forms, specific drug substances, route/dosage form changes
Waiver of in vivo BE studies may be granted for: solutions, parenteral solutions, BCS Class 1 drugs meeting dissolution criteria
Hierarchy of BE approaches: (1) PK studies in humans, (2) PD studies, (3) clinical endpoint studies, (4) in vitro studies
Analytical methods MUST be validated for accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility
Adverse drug experience reporting for marketed Rx drugs without approved NDAs: serious/unexpected within 15 days; fatal/life-threatening within 7 days; records retained 10 years
Key definitions: biological product, safety, purity, potency, sterility, dating period, expiration date, complete response letter
Personnel SHALL have capabilities commensurate with functions; sterile operations personnel SHALL wear clean/sterilized protective clothing
Work areas SHALL be clean/orderly; sterilization minimum 121.5C/20min saturated steam or 170C/2hr dry heat; live vaccine areas require separate/dedicated facilities
Manufacturing records SHALL be concurrent; retained minimum 5 years or 6 months after expiration (whichever later); sterilization records by automatic devices
Retention samples SHALL be kept for at least 6 months after expiration date
Biological product deviation reporting: within 45 calendar days of discovery on Form FDA-3486
Product-specific shipment temperature table (e.g., live vaccines <=10C, Fresh Frozen Plasma <=-18C, Whole Blood 1-10C)
Postmarketing AE reporting: 15-day alert for serious/unexpected; quarterly for 3 years then annual; 10-year recordkeeping; electronic format required
Manufacturing discontinuance notification: 6 months prior or 5 business days if not feasible

BLA SHALL contain: nonclinical/clinical data demonstrating safety/purity/potency; GLP statement per Part 58; IRB/informed consent statements; financial disclosure per Part 54; manufacturing description; stability data; samples; labels
Complete Response Letter: applicant MUST resubmit or withdraw within 1 year
Changes to approved BLA: Major=Prior Approval Supplement; Moderate=CBE-30; Minor=Annual Report; assessment of effects on identity/strength/quality/purity/potency MUST be demonstrated
Electronic format submissions MUST be in form FDA can process, review, and archive
BLA approved ONLY after establishment inspection; must comply with GMP Parts 210/211/600/606/820
Pediatric studies required for new active ingredient, indication, dosage form, dosing regimen, or route
Accelerated Approval: surrogate endpoint approval with mandatory postmarketing confirmatory studies; withdrawal if studies fail to verify benefit
Annual progress reports for postmarketing studies due within 60 days of approval anniversary
Animal Rule: approval based on animal efficacy when human studies not ethical/feasible; requires >1 animal species, PK/PD data, postmarketing verification
Personnel SHALL be adequate in number, education, training, and experience
Facilities SHALL provide adequate quarantine storage, processing, and labeling areas
Equipment calibration per mandated frequency table; sterilization minimum 121.5C/20min or 170C/2hr
SOPs MUST cover 14 mandatory topic areas for all blood collection/processing/testing/distribution steps

guidance_recommendations.note: FDA guidance documents represent the Agency's current thinking and are NOT legally binding. Alternative approaches may be used if they satisfy applicable statutes and regulations.

key_thresholds.significant_change: 5% assay change from initial, or any degradation product exceeding criterion
key_thresholds.testing_frequency: Every 3 months year 1, every 6 months year 2, annually thereafter

key_thresholds.case_1s_highest_risk: Inhalation/injection: USP Biological Reactivity + extraction + tox evaluation + batch monitoring
key_thresholds.case_4s_lowest_risk: Oral tablets/capsules: indirect food additive reference sufficient
key_thresholds.prohibition: Postconsumer recycled plastic prohibited for primary containers

key_thresholds.stage_1: Process Design — DOE studies recommended; early experiments need not be CGMP compliant
key_thresholds.stage_2: Process Performance Qualification — CGMP required; must complete before commercial distribution
key_thresholds.stage_3: Continued Process Verification — ongoing per 21 CFR 211.180(e)

key_thresholds.BCS_class_1_biowaiver: >=85% dissolved in 15 minutes at pH 1.2, 4.5, and 6.8
key_thresholds.BCS_class_3_biowaiver: >=85% dissolved in 15 minutes; excipients qualitatively same, quantitatively similar to RLD

key_thresholds.standard: 90% CI for AUC and Cmax ratio within 80.00-125.00%
key_thresholds.NTI_drugs: 90% CI within 90.00-111.11% for both AUC and Cmax
key_thresholds.highly_variable: Reference-scaled BE for Cmax when CV >= 30%; max widening 69.84-143.19%; GMR must remain 80-125%

key_thresholds.screening_cut_point: 95% of drug-naive healthy donors classified as negative
key_thresholds.particle_concern_range: 2-10 micron subvisible particles of particular concern

key_thresholds.prespecification: All adaptive design details must be prespecified before trial initiation
key_thresholds.data_access: Comparative interim results limited to independent personnel

key_thresholds.submission_timing: 60 days after EOP2 meeting or 210 days before marketing application

key_thresholds.accelerated_approval: Surrogate endpoint + mandatory postmarketing confirmatory study

binding_regulations.document: Guideline on Investigation of Bioequivalence (CPMP/EWP/QWP/1401/98 Rev.1)

requirements.description: 90% CI for AUC(0-t) and Cmax must fall within 80.00-125.00%
Minimum 12 evaluable subjects required
Test batch must be >= 1/10 of production scale or 100,000 units (whichever larger)
NTID drugs: AUC 90% CI within 90.00-111.11%
HVDP (CV>=30%): Cmax interval may widen to 69.84-143.19% using scaled average BE with replicate crossover
In vitro dissolution profiling required at pH 1.2, 4.5, 6.8
Washout period must be >= 5 elimination half-lives

biowaivers.BCS_Class_I: Accepted with high solubility + high permeability + rapid dissolution
biowaivers.BCS_Class_III: Accepted with conditions (high solubility + low permeability + very rapid dissolution + similar excipients)

binding_regulations.document: Biosimilars Non-Clinical and Clinical Issues (EMEA/CHMP/BMWP/42832/2005 Rev1)

requirements.description: Stepwise comparability exercise: Step 1 (in vitro) -> Step 2 (justify in vivo need) -> Step 3 (in vivo only if residual uncertainty)
PK comparability study required (single-dose crossover preferred)
Immunogenicity: minimum 1-year follow-up pre-authorization for chronic-use products
Risk Management Plan (RMP) mandatory at submission
Foreign manufacturers MUST obtain accreditation from MHLW prior to manufacturing for Japan import
5-year mandatory renewal cycle; failure = loss of accreditation
Notification of changes within 30 days (PAL Article 100)
1 rodent species long-term study (rat preferred, 24-30 months) PLUS 1 short/medium-term alternative system
Minimum 50 animals/sex/group, 3 dose levels + control
High-dose AUC must be >= 25x human AUC at maximum therapeutic dose
Limit dose: 1,500 mg/kg/day when human dose <= 500 mg/day
Biomarker qualification submissions MUST use CTD/eCTD structure
Context of Use (COU) must be explicitly defined and scientifically justified
Orphan designation grants priority review and re-examination period up to 10 years
15% R&D tax credit on designated orphan product development expenditures
